CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 10.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO PLEAD A STATUTORY BASIS FOR LIABILITY AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION AND TO DELETE REFERENCE TO BOVIS CONSTRUCTION AS AN EMPLOYEE OF DEFENDANT OR PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THAT BOVIS CONSTRUCTION WAS AN EMPLOYEE OF DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PRAYER FOR DESTRUCTION OF, LOSS OF VALUE, LOSS OF USE, OR REPAIR OF REAL PROPERTY AND THE PRAYER FOR GENERAL DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD A PROPERTY INTEREST IN THE REAL PROPERTY AT ISSUE AND GENERAL DAMAGES IN CONNECTION WITH THE NUISANCE CAUSE OF ACTION. GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PRAYERS FOR BONUS VALUE OF LEASEHOLD, PRECONDEMNATION DAMAGES, AND LOSS OF GOODWILL BUT WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF FILING AN AMENDED COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE SUCH PRAYERS IF THERE LATER BECOMES A FACTUAL BASIS TO DO SO. DENIED ON ALL OTHER GROUNDS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael C. Wenzel, Esq., of Bertrand Fox & Elliot, Tel. 415.353.0999, appearing for defendant and moving party.
James Treppa, Esq., of Bledsoe, Tel. 415.981.5411, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the first cause of action and sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action. Plaintiff is given leave to plead a statutory basis for liability as to the second and third causes of action and to delete reference to Bovis Construction as an employee of Defendant or plead facts showing that Bovis Construction was an employee of Defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael C. Wenzel, Esq., of Bertrand Fox & Elliot, Tel. 415.353.0999, appearing for defendant and moving party.
James Treppa, Esq., of Bledsoe, Tel. 415.981.5411, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted in part and denied in part. Granted with leave to amend as to the prayer for destruction of, loss of value, loss of use, or repair of real property and the prayer for general damages. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead a property interest in the real property at issue and general damages in connection with the nuisance cause of action. Granted without leave to amend as to the prayers for bonus value of leasehold, precondemnation damages, and loss of goodwill but without prejudice to Plaintiff filing an amended complaint to allege such prayers if there later becomes a factual basis to do so. Denied on all other grounds. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
